hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — Ace, 10, Jack. You bet. Your opponent called.
Turn — King. You bet. Your opponent called again.
River — 9. Your opponent led out with a confident bet.

wrongReasons: 8 9 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 8 9 would have.
6 Q makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
5 3 has little reason to lead the river here — high card can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 7 9

Your opponent called two streets with nothing but overcards, then the river card paired one of their hole cards. The sudden lead on the river — after calling twice — is the tell: they finally hit something and went for value.
